---
title: "Teaching"
---

I teach economics at [School of Economics](https://economie.uca.fr/) and [School of Law](https://droit.uca.fr/) of Université Clermont Auvergne, mainly at the undergraduate and master's levels. My teaching covers introductory economics, economic history, macroeconomics, environmental economics, institutions, and territorial development.

A substantial part of my teaching has involved large undergraduate cohorts,
including lectures in introductory economics delivered to several hundred
students. I have developed online learning resources and self-assessment materials for my courses accessible on Moodle.

## Current teaching

### Undergraduate courses

- **Introduction to Economics**  
  First-year Economics and Management programme.

- **Economic History**  
  First-year Economics, Administration and Social Sciences, and Management
  programmes.

- **Macroeconomics**  
  Second-year Administration and Social Sciences programme.

- **Political Economy**  
  Second-year *Capacité en droit* programme.

- **Sustainable Territorial Policies**  
  Undergraduate elective course on ecological taxation, social and solidarity
  economy, and territorial transitions.

- **Ecological and Territorial Transitions**  
  Third-year undergraduate course in the Administration and Social Sciences
  programme.

### Graduate courses

- **Institutional Economics**  
  Master's programme in Development Economics, track *Economics and
  Territorial Transitions*.

- **Environmental Economics - Economic valuation**  
  Master's programmes in Economic Policy Management (GPE) and Project
  Management for Development (MODEV), in partnership with FERDI and AFD.

- **Territorial Public Policy**  
  Master's programme in Economics and Territorial Transitions.

- **Applied Seminars and Territorial Engineering**  
  Master's programme in Economics and Territorial Transitions.

## Programme responsibilities

I am Co-Director of the Master's track in [**Economics and Territorial
Transitions**](https://economie.uca.fr/formation/master/master-economie-du-developpement-parc-economie-territoriale-nee) at Université Clermont Auvergne. The programme trains students to analyse, design, implement, and evaluate territorial public policies.

Its curriculum combines economic analysis with applied projects carried out in partnership with local governments and public actors. I supervise internships and collective projects involving local development, ecological transition, and public policy evaluation.

From 2013 to 2021, I was Head of the undergraduate degree in **Administration
and Social Sciences** at the School of Law of Université Clermont Auvergne.

I also coordinate the **Environment and Economic Policy** module in the [GPE
Master's programme](https://ihedd.ferdi.fr/formations/master-gestion-de-la-politique-economique-gpe), organised in partnership with FERDI and involving
researchers and practitioners working on environmental and climate policy.

## Professional training on ecological transition

Since 2024, I have served as Scientific Coordinator for a professional
training programme delivered by the [IHEdd](https://ihedd.ferdi.fr/) and FERDI on quantitative tools for ecological transition and public policy.

The programme, *Piloting the Ecological Transition: Practical Tools for Public Action*, is designed for public-sector practitioners and policy professionals. It combines an online introductory module with in-person training sessions.

It provides participants with a practical understanding of three complementary families of decision-support tools:

- environmental-economic accounting and natural capital accounts;
- monetary valuation of environmental goods, services, and policy impacts;
- integrated macro-environmental models, including GEMMES and ESTEEM.

As Scientific Coordinator, I design the overall pedagogical structure, coordinate contributions from academic and policy experts, and teach sessions on monetary valuation. The programme connects economic analysis with concrete policy issues, including climate planning, public investment, environmental budgeting, and the assessment of ecological-transition policies.
